title: Fibrous mats and panels having a gypsum-based coating and methods for the manufacture thereof
abstract: Coated nonwoven fibrous mats, building panels, and methods for making the same are provided. A coated nonwoven fiber mat includes a nonwoven fibrous mat having a continuous barrier coating on a surface thereof. The continuous barrier coating includes a set composition of gypsum and a binder. A building panel includes a panel core associated with a coated nonwoven fiber mat. A method of making a coated nonwoven fibrous mat includes depositing a composition of water, gypsum stucco, and a binder onto a surface of a nonwoven fibrous mat and setting the composition to form a continuous barrier coating.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A building panel, comprising: a panel core associated with a first nonwoven fibrous mat; a continuous barrier coating on a surface of the first nonwoven fibrous mat opposite the panel core, the continuous barrier coating comprising a set composition that comprises gypsum and a binder. 2. The building panel of claim 1, wherein the panel core comprises one or more core layers. 3. The building panel of claim 2, wherein one or more core layers comprise at least one slate coat layer. 4. The building panel of claim 1, wherein the panel core comprises one or more layer compositions. 5. The building panel of claim 4, wherein the panel core comprises one or more gypsum layer compositions. 6. The building panel of claim 4, wherein the panel core comprises one or more polyisocyanurate or polyurethane layer compositions. 7. The building panel of claim 1, wherein the panel core comprises foamed or low-density polymeric materials. 8. The building panel of claim 1, wherein the panel core comprises cementitious materials. 9. The building panel of claim 1, wherein the panel core comprises inorganic hollow spheres. 10. The building panel of claim 1, wherein the panel core comprises expanded or exfoliated low-density inorganic materials. 11. The building panel of claim 1, wherein the panel core comprises inorganic materials and an inorganic binder. 12. The building panel of claim 1, where the panel core comprises inorganic materials and a polymer binder. 13. The building panel of claim 1, wherein the first nonwoven fibrous mat is a nonwoven fiberglass mat. 14. The building panel of claim 1, wherein the first nonwoven fibrous mat comprises fiberglass and polymer fiber. 15. The building panel of claim 1, wherein the first nonwoven fibrous mat is a nonwoven polymeric fiber mat. 16. The building panel of claim 1, wherein the binder is derived from a polymer emulsion or resin composition. 17. The building panel of claim 1, wherein the gypsum is derived from gypsum hemihydrate. 18. The building panel of claim 1, wherein the coating composition further comprises one or more additives selected from a group consisting of glass fiber, polymer fiber, dispersant, water repellent, biocide, defoamer, viscosity modifier, colorant, accelerator, retarder, inorganic hollow spheres, mineral platelets, and inorganic fillers. 19. The building panel of claim 1, wherein the coating weighs between 2 lbs per 100 square feet and 8.5 lbs per 100 square feet, on a dry basis. 20. The building panel of claim 1, further comprising: a polymer coating on a surface of the continuous barrier coating opposite the first nonwoven fibrous mat, the polymer coating being derived from a latex emulsion or resin composition. 21. The building panel of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the coating has an average depth of penetration into the fibrous mat between about 10% and about 70% of a thickness of the first nonwoven fibrous mat. 22. A coated nonwoven fibrous mat, comprising: a nonwoven fibrous mat having a continuous barrier coating on a surface thereof, wherein the continuous barrier coating comprises a set composition comprising gypsum and a binder. 23. The coated nonwoven fibrous mat of claim 22, wherein nonwoven fibrous mat is a nonwoven fiberglass mat. 24. The coated nonwoven fibrous mat of claim 22, where nonwoven fibrous mat is a polymeric fiber mat. 25. The coated nonwoven fibrous mat of claim 22, where nonwoven fibrous mat is a hybrid nonwoven mat comprising fiberglass and polymeric fiber. 26. The coated nonwoven fibrous mat of claim 22, where nonwoven fibrous mat contains carbon fibers. 27. The coated nonwoven fibrous mat of claim 22, wherein the binder is derived from a polymer latex emulsion or resin composition. 28. The coated nonwoven fibrous mat of claim 22, wherein the gypsum is derived from gypsum hemihydrate. 29. The coated nonwoven fibrous mat of claim 22, wherein the coating composition further comprises one or more additives selected from a group consisting of glass fiber, polymer fiber, dispersant, water repellent, biocide, defoamer, viscosity modifier, colorant, accelerator, retarder, inorganic hollow spheres, mineral platelets, and inorganic fillers. 30. The coated nonwoven fibrous mat of claim 22, further comprising: a polymer coating on a surface of the continuous barrier coating opposite the nonwoven fibrous mat, the polymer coating being derived from a polymer latex emulsion or resin composition. 31. The coated nonwoven fibrous mat of claim 22, wherein the coated nonwoven fibrous mat displays a Gurley porosity of at least 2 seconds. 32. The coated nonwoven fibrous mat of claim 22, wherein the coating weighs between about 2 lbs per 100 square feet and about 8.5 lbs per 100 square feet on a dry basis. 33. The coated nonwoven fibrous mat of any one of claims 22 to 32, wherein the coating has an average depth of penetration into the fibrous mat between about 10% and about 70% of a thickness of the nonwoven fibrous mat. 34. A method of making a coated nonwoven fibrous mat, comprising: depositing a continuous layer of coating slurry that comprises a composition of water, gypsum, and a binder onto a first surface of a nonwoven fibrous mat; and setting or curing the composition to form a solid, continuous coating on the first surface of the nonwoven fibrous mat. 35. The method of claim 34, wherein nonwoven fibrous mat is a nonwoven fiberglass mat. 36. The method of claim 34, wherein the binder comprises a polymer latex emulsion or resin composition. 37. The method of claim 34, wherein the gypsum is gypsum hemihydrate. 38. The method of claim 34, wherein depositing the continuous layer of slurry is carried out using a knife coater, curtain coater, roll coater, metering rod coater, slot die coater, gravure coating, immersion coater or a combination thereof. 39. The method of claim 34, wherein the coating slurry composition comprises gypsum hemihydrate in an amount of from about 10 to about 95 percent, by dry weight of the composition, and binder in an amount of from about 5 to about 30 percent, by dry weight of the composition. 40. The method of claim 34, wherein the coating slurry composition further comprises one or more additives selected from a group consisting of glass fiber, polymer fiber, dispersant, water repellent, biocide, defoamer, viscosity modifier, colorant, accelerator, retarder, inorganic hollow spheres, mineral platelets, and inorganic fillers. 41. The method of claim 34, wherein the coated nonwoven fibrous mat displays a Gurley porosity of at least 2 seconds. 42. The method of claim 34, wherein the coated nonwoven fibrous mat has a coating weight in the range between about 2 lbs per 100 square feet and about 8.5 lbs per 100 square feet, on a dry basis. 43. The method of any one of claims 34 to 42, wherein the coating has an average depth of penetration into the fibrous mat between about 10% and about 70% of a thickness of the nonwoven fibrous mat. 44. A method of making a building panel, comprising: providing a nonwoven fibrous mat having a continuous barrier coating on a first surface, the continuous barrier coating comprising set gypsum and a binder; depositing a panel core material onto a second surface of the nonwoven fibrous mat opposite the first surface; providing a facing material onto a surface of the panel core opposite the coated nonwoven fibrous mat, and setting or curing the panel core material to form a panel. 45. The method of claim 44, wherein providing the nonwoven fibrous mat comprises: depositing a continuous layer of slurry that comprises a composition of water, gypsum hemihydrate, and a binder onto the first surface of the nonwoven fibrous mat; and setting or curing the composition to form a solid, continuous barrier coating on the first surface of the nonwoven fibrous mat. 46. The method of claim 44, wherein depositing the panel core material comprises depositing one or more gypsum core layers. 47. The method of claim 46, wherein depositing one or more gypsum layers comprises depositing at least one slate coat layer. 48. The method of claim 44, wherein nonwoven fibrous mat is a nonwoven fiberglass mat. 49. The method of claim 44, wherein the binder is derived from a polymer latex or resin composition. 50. The method of claim 44, wherein the coating comprises gypsum dihydrate in an amount of from about 33 to about 95 percent, by dry weight of the composition, and binder in an amount of from about 5 to about 30 percent, by dry weight of the composition. 51. The method of claim 44, wherein the coating further comprises one or more additives selected from a group consisting of glass fiber, polymer fiber, dispersant, water repellent, biocide, defoamer, viscosity modifier, accelerator, retarder, inorganic hollow spheres, mineral platelets, inorganic fillers, and colorant. 52. The method of claim 44, further comprising: depositing a polymer coating on a surface of the continuous barrier coating opposite the nonwoven fibrous mat, the polymer coating comprising a polymer latex or resin. 53. The method of claim 44, wherein depositing the panel core material comprises depositing one or more polyisocyanurate layers. 54. The method of any one of claims 44 to 53, wherein the coated nonwoven fibrous mat has a coating weight in the range between about 2 lbs per 100 square feet and about 8.5 lbs per 100 square feet, on a dry basis. 55. A method of making a building panel, comprising: adhering a panel core material to a second surface of a nonwoven fibrous mat, or vice versa; depositing a continuous layer of coating slurry that comprises a composition of water, gypsum hemihydrate, and a binder onto a first surface of the nonwoven fibrous mat, opposite the second surface, and setting or curing the panel core material and the slurry composition to form a panel having a solid, continuous barrier coating on the first surface of the nonwoven fibrous mat. 56. The method of claim 55, wherein the panel core material comprises one or more panel core layers. 57. The method of claim 56, wherein the panel core material comprises at least one slate coat layer. 58. The method of claim 55, wherein nonwoven fibrous mat is a nonwoven fiberglass mat. 59. The method of claim 55, wherein the binder comprises a polymer latex emulsion or resin composition. 60. The method of claim 55, wherein the composition comprises gypsum hemihydrate in an amount of from about 10 to about 95 percent, by dry weight of the composition, and binder in an amount of from about 5 to about 30 percent, by dry weight of the composition. 61. The method of claim 55, wherein the composition further comprises one or more additives selected from a group consisting of glass fiber, polymer fiber, dispersant, water repellent, biocide, defoamer, viscosity modifier, accelerator, retarder, inorganic hollow spheres, mineral platelets, inorganic fillers, and colorant. 62. The method of claim 55, wherein the panel core material comprises one or more polyisocyanurate layers. 63. The method of claim 55, further comprising: depositing a polymer coating on a surface of the continuous barrier coating opposite the nonwoven fibrous mat, the polymer coating comprising a latex emulsion or resin. 64. The method of any one of claims 55 to 63, wherein the continuous barrier coating weighs between about 2 lbs per 100 square feet and about 10 lbs per 100 square feet, on a dry basis.
